question.question: The sandbox report contains signatures rated at different threat levels: malicious (threat_level: 2), suspicious (threat_level: 1), and informative (threat_level: informative). Based ONLY on signatures rated as malicious (threat_level: 2), which of the following behaviours were observed in this detonation?
options: A. A spawned process (svchost.exe) running from %APPDATA% was identified as malicious by antivirus vendors
B. The malware attempted to sleep for more than two minutes to evade sandbox timing analysis
C. The malware wrote data to a remote process using process injection
D. The malware created guarded memory regions as an anti-debugging technique
E. The malware deleted volume shadow copies using vssadmin.exe
F. The malware accessed browser sensitive information files
G. The malware wrote files with double extensions, indicative of ransomware encryption
H. The malware deleted the Windows backup catalog using wbadmin.exe
I. The malware created a fake system process by running svchost.exe from a non-standard path
J. The malware adjusted debug privileges (SE_DEBUG_PRIVILEGE)
